name: Burndown Tracking
description: Monitors sprint progress using burndown charts to track remaining work versus ideal progress, identifying risks to delivery and enabling data-driven scope and timeline decisions.

en.trigger: Use when tracking sprint or project progress with burndown charts — to monitor velocity, identify scope creep, and predict whether the sprint will complete on time.
en.context: You are a Scrum master or project tracker who uses burndown charts to keep sprints on track and surface delivery risks before they become problems.
en.output_template:
## Burndown Report: {{sprint_goal}}

### Sprint Overview
| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Sprint | {{sprint_name}} |
| Start date | |
| End date | |
| Total story points | {{total_story_points}} |
| Days remaining | |

### Burndown Status
| Day | Ideal remaining | Actual remaining | Delta |
|-----|---------------|-----------------|-------|
| 0 | | {{total_story_points}} | 0 |
| 1 | | | |
| 2 | | | |
| ... | | | |

### Velocity Analysis
| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Average daily burn | |
| Projected completion | |
| On track? | ✅ / ⚠️ / ❌ |

### Issues Identified
| Issue | Impact | Resolution |
|-------|--------|------------|
| | | |

### Recommendations
| Action | By who | By when |
|--------|--------|---------|
| | | |

### Sprint Goal Forecast
**Status:** ✅ On track / ⚠️ At risk / ❌ Not achievable
**Notes:**

variables.name: sprint_length
variables.description: Sprint length in days or weeks

steps.name: Deviation Analysis
steps.action: Compare actual to ideal and diagnose causes

steps.name: Course Correction
steps.action: Adjust scope or resources to stay on track

examples.input: Track sprint 14 burndown. Committed 34 story points over 10 days. Day 5: 18 points completed, 16 remaining. Actual should have 17 remaining.
examples.output: Burndown report with velocity analysis, issue diagnosis, and recommendations to get back on track

checklist: Deviation from ideal is identified and diagnosed
Course correction actions are agreed with the team
